description: The IP address of the actor who initiated the tracked event.
A flag indicating whether the actor was anonymous (not logged in) when the tracked event occurred.
The MlsId of the user who initiated the tracked event.
The user name of the actor who initiated the tracked event.
The total number of bathrooms in the property as an integer (full bathrooms; halves counted in BathroomsHalf).
The total number of bedrooms in the property.
The model name or model number of the builder's plan for the property (project / development name).
The name of the builder of the property (developer / seller company for new builds).
The MemberMlsId of the member who made the change.
The type of change recorded. RESO standard lookup values: Add, Modify, Delete.
The city in which the property is located.
A system-unique identifier for the contact. Often a number, but can be a string.

The Contacts resource holds people in an MLS user's personal address book — buyers, sellers, prospects, lenders, vendors. Contacts contain PII (name, email, phone) and are NOT publicly broadcast; access is restricted to the owning Member.
The type of contact. RESO standard lookup values include Buyer, Seller, Renter, Landlord, Vendor, Other.
The country in which the property is located, as an ISO country code.

The ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which monetary fields on the listing are denominated (e.g. EUR, USD, GBP). Affects ListPrice, OriginalListPrice, LeaseAmount, and other monetary amounts on the Property record.
The development phase of the property. Common values: Proposed, Under Construction, New Construction, Existing.
The email address of the contact.
The date and time the tracked event occurred (ISO 8601).
The type of event being tracked. Common values: PageView, Search, Save, Share, ContactRequest, Click.
The name of the field that was changed in the resource record.
The first or given name of the contact.
The full name of the contact - first middle last suffix.

The HistoryTransactional resource is the field-level audit log for changes made to other RESO resources. Each row records the resource, record id, field name, change type (Add/Modify/Delete), previous and new value, and the Member who made the change.

The InternetTracking resource records consumer-facing engagement events on syndicated listings — page views, searches, saves, shares, contact requests, clicks. Events identify the object, the actor (or anonymous flag), and the event timestamp for analytics + reporting.
The last or surname of the contact.
The geographic latitude of the property in decimal degrees. Positive values are north of the equator; negative values are south. Combined with Longitude to position the property on a map.
The current lease amount of the property as a single value or a periodic payment per LeaseAmountFrequency.
The frequency of the lease amount. RESO lookup: Annually, Monthly, SeeRemarks, Weekly.

A flag indicating that the seller would consider leasing the property in addition to a sale. mls_2_0 sets this TRUE on the sale primary record of a Cyprus dual sale-and-rent listing; the sibling lease record uses ListingKey = <primary>_LEASE.

Atlas-derived classification bucket (Listing / Marketing / Rent) computed from the canonical RESO fields ListingService, ListingAgreement, LeaseConsideredYN and SaleConsideredYN. This is NOT a RESO Data Dictionary field — it exists only inside Atlas to drive the operational 3-bucket chip filter on the listings index.
The full name of the listing agent of record.
A system-unique identifier for the listing agent. References Member.MemberKey.

The type of contractual relationship between the listing agent and the seller. Values: ExclusiveAgency, ExclusiveRightToLease, ExclusiveRightToSell, ExclusiveRightWithException, Net, Open, Probate. mls_2_0 emits ExclusiveAgency only when the source proves it; otherwise Open or NULL.
The well-known identifier for the listing, also known as the MLS number.
A system-unique identifier for the listing. Mls 2.0 prefixes Cyprus listings with QOBRIX_ and the lease sibling of a dual sale-and-rent listing with the suffix _LEASE.

The level of service the listing broker is providing to the seller. Common values: FullService (broker actively engaged, full MLS service), LimitedService (some services only), EntryOnly (listing entered into the MLS but no broker representation).
The unique identifier of the brokerage Office responsible for marketing the listing. Foreign key into the Office resource; pairs with ListAgentKey to identify the listing brokerage and listing agent.
The current price of the property as listed by the seller.
The total interior living area of the property, measured in LivingAreaUnits (typically SquareMeters or SquareFeet).
The geographic longitude of the property in decimal degrees. Positive values are east of the prime meridian; negative values are west. Combined with Latitude to position the property on a map.
The size of the lot in square feet (in CDL we honor LotSizeUnits and accept SquareMeters too).

The Media resource describes images, videos, virtual tours, documents, and other media items associated with a Property. Each media item carries a MediaURL, MediaCategory, and ordering information so consumers can render galleries in the intended sequence.
The category of the media object. RESO standard lookup values: Photo, Video, VirtualTour, FloorPlan, Document, Audio, OtherAerial, AgentPhoto, OfficePhoto, etc.
A system-unique identifier for the media object. Often a number, but can be a string.
A URL pointing to the actual media object (image, video, document, virtual tour, etc.).

The Member resource describes a person who is a member of the MLS — most commonly a real estate agent or broker. Members are referenced from Property via ListAgentKey / CoListAgentKey, and are responsible for creating, updating, or being assigned to listings.
The email address of the member.
The full name of the member - first middle last suffix. The contents of this field may be the result of concatenating the parts of a member's name into a complete name.
A system-unique identifier for the member. Often a number, but can be a string. Foreign-keyed to Member.MemberKey from Property.ListAgentKey, OpenHouse.ListAgentKey, etc.
The local, well-known identifier for the member as assigned by the MLS. Member.MemberMlsId is unique per OriginatingSystem.
The phone number that the member prefers to be contacted at.
The status of the member's record in the MLS or other organization. Common lookup values: Active, Inactive.
The type of member. RESO standard lookup values: Office Manager, Salesperson, Broker, Appraiser, Office Staff, Office Broker, Designated REALTOR, REALTOR, Office Manager, Affiliate, MLS Only.
The mobile phone number of the contact.
The date and time the listing was last modified, formatted as ISO 8601.
A flag indicating that the property has never been previously occupied. Primary industry filter for new-vs-resale.
The new value of the field after the change.
The MlsId (e.g. Property.ListingId) of the object the tracked event applies to.
The type of object the tracked event applies to (Property, Member, Office, OpenHouse, etc.).
The full name of the current occupant of the Property. Distinct from OwnerName when the Property is tenant-occupied or vacant-with-caretaker.
The current occupant's contact phone number. Used for showing coordination when the occupant is not the owner (tenant, property manager).
A single-value lookup describing who currently occupies the Property: Owner | Tenant | Vacant. Atlas enforces this enum via a CHECK constraint on properties.occupant_type.

The Office resource describes a brokerage office. Offices employ Members and are referenced from Property via ListOfficeKey / CoListOfficeKey. Each office has identity, contact information, and a designated broker.
The first address line of the physical address of the office.
The MemberMlsId of the broker of record for the office.
The city of the physical address of the office.
The country of the physical address of the office (ISO country code).
The contact email address of the office.
A system-unique identifier for the office. Often a number, but can be a string. Foreign-keyed from Member.OfficeKey, Property.ListOfficeKey, etc.
The local, well-known identifier for the office as assigned by the MLS.
The legal or DBA name of the office.
The main contact phone number of the office.
The status of the office's record in the MLS or other organization. Common lookup values: Active, Inactive.

The OpenHouse resource describes a scheduled event during which a Property is open for public or broker viewing. Each open house has a date, start/end time, status, and an optional remark; multiple open houses can be scheduled per Property.
The date of the open house event.
The end date and time of the open house event (ISO 8601).
A system-unique identifier for the open house event.
Free-form remarks describing the open house event (refreshments, parking instructions, etc.).
The start date and time of the open house event (ISO 8601).
The status of the open house event. RESO standard lookup values: Active, Cancelled, Ended.
The type of the open house event. RESO standard lookup values: Public, BrokerOpen, OfficeOnly, Virtual.
The display order of the media object relative to other media objects of the same resource record. Lower = displayed first.
The original listing price of the property as it was first listed.
A system-unique identifier for the member that owns the contact record (References Member.MemberKey).

The full legal name of the Property owner. Free-text scalar; the canonical owner identifier on the Property resource. RESO does not define an owner FK; same person across multiple listings is matched by name.
The Property owner's contact phone number. Free-text; format is not constrained by RESO so jurisdictions may store local conventions (e.g. +357 for Cyprus).
Free-text description of the legal ownership type of the Property: Sole Owner, Trust, Corporation, LLC, Joint Tenancy, etc. Used for due-diligence disclosures and title searches.
The postal code in which the property is located.
The annual energy production of the installation in kWh. Combined with PowerProductionAnnualStatus to indicate whether the value is estimated or actual.
A single-value lookup describing the source of PowerProductionAnnual: Estimated | Actual | Other.
The unique identifier for a power-production installation within the originating system. Primary key of the PropertyPowerProduction resource.
A single-value lookup describing the ownership model: Owned | Leased | LeasedAssumable | Other. Affects whether the system transfers with the sale.
The nameplate capacity of the installation (typically kW).
A single-value lookup describing the energy source: Photovoltaics | Wind. Future RESO revisions may add Geothermal, Hydro, etc.
The four-digit year the system was installed. Used to estimate remaining warranty life and degradation curve.
The phone number that the contact prefers to be contacted at.
The previous value of the field before the change.

The Property resource describes the real estate parcel for sale or lease. It contains the bulk of the listing data: identity, status, pricing, structure, location, and the agents/offices marketing it. Atlas exposes Property as the canonical listing record on which all other resources hang.
A list of property conditions describing the state of the property. Multi-value CSV. Standard tokens: Fixer, New Construction, To Be Built, Under Construction, Updated/Remodeled.
A system-unique identifier for the property the media object is attached to. References Property.ListingKey (or the relevant resource's primary key).

The PropertyPowerProduction resource describes on-site renewable energy production installations attached to a Property (solar photovoltaics, wind, ...). Each row carries the system type, ownership (Owned/Leased/...), nameplate capacity (kW), annual production (kWh), and install year.

The PropertyRooms resource describes individual rooms within a Property: type (Bedroom/Bathroom/Kitchen/...), level (Basement/First/Second/...), dimensions, area, and room features. One Property has many PropertyRooms rows linked via ListingKey.
A more specific classification of the property within PropertyType (e.g. Apartment, SingleFamilyResidence, Villa, Office). Open list - tenants may extend.
The high-level classification of the property: Residential, ResidentialIncome, ResidentialLease, CommercialSale, CommercialLease, Land, ManufacturedInPark, BusinessOpportunity, Farm.

The PropertyUnitTypes resource describes the different unit types within a multi-unit Property (Studio, 1 Bedroom, 2 Bedroom, Penthouse, ...). Each row carries beds/baths/units totals plus rent and description for that unit type. Critical for new-construction developments where a single project listing fans into many sellable unit types.
The publicly visible marketing description of the property, intended for consumer-facing portals and the IDX feed. Free-form text; should not contain contact information per most MLS rules.
The name of the RESO resource the change record is for (Property, Member, Office, etc.).
The well-known identifier of the resource record the change record is for (e.g. Property.ListingId).
The numeric area of the room. Units are jurisdiction-dependent (m2 in EU, sqft in US).
Free-text description of the room. Used for listing-side narrative when dimensions/features are not enough.
The room's dimensions as a free-text string (typically formatted '<length>x<width>'). Use the numeric RoomLength / RoomWidth / RoomArea fields when machine-readable values are required.
A multi-value lookup of features present in the room: Ceiling Fan | Walk-in Closet | En-suite Bathroom | Hardwood Floor | Tile Floor | Built-in Shelving | ...
The unique identifier for a room within the originating system. Primary key of the PropertyRooms resource.
The numeric length of the room. Units are jurisdiction-dependent (typically meters in EU markets, feet in US).
A single-value lookup describing which floor or level the room is on: Basement | First | Second | Third | Lower | Upper | Main | Loft | ...
A single-value lookup describing the type of room: Bedroom | Bathroom | Kitchen | LivingRoom | DiningRoom | Office | Laundry | Bonus | ...
The numeric width of the room. Units are jurisdiction-dependent (typically meters in EU markets, feet in US).
A flag indicating that the lessor would consider selling the property in addition to a lease. mls_2_0 sets this TRUE on the lease sibling record of a Cyprus dual sale-and-rent listing.
A short caption / description of the media object, typically displayed below the image in galleries.
The MemberMlsId of the agent who is showing the property.

The ShowingAppointment resource describes a scheduled in-person showing of a Property by a buyer's agent. It captures the appointment time window, the showing agent, the appointment status, and how the appointment was made (Method).
The end date and time of the showing appointment (ISO 8601).
A system-unique identifier for the showing appointment.
The way the showing appointment was scheduled or will be conducted. Common values: GoAndShow, CallListAgent, CallSeller, Online, Lockbox, etc.
The start date and time of the showing appointment (ISO 8601).
The status of the showing appointment. Common values: Requested, Approved, Confirmed, Cancelled, Denied, Completed, NoShow.

The MLS-defined statuses normalized into a common set. The status of the listing as it relates to MLS activities. Common values: Active, Pending, Closed, Withdrawn, Expired, Canceled, Hold, Incomplete, Delete.
The state or province in which the property is located.

The Teams resource describes a group of Members operating together within an Office. Teams have a team lead, a name, and contact information; their Members co-list and co-show properties on behalf of the team brand.
The contact email address of the team.
A system-unique identifier for the team. Often a number, but can be a string.
The MemberMlsId of the lead member of the team.
The local, well-known identifier for the team as assigned by the MLS.
The name of the team as established by the team's leader and members.
The status of the team's record in the MLS or other organization. Common lookup values: Active, Inactive.
Current rent per unit of this type (numeric). For sales projects this may carry the current sale price per unit instead.
Number of bathrooms per unit of this type. Numeric to allow half-baths (e.g. 2.5).
Number of bedrooms per unit of this type.
Free-text description of the unit type for marketing copy.
A single-value lookup: Furnished | Partially | Unfurnished.
Number of garage spaces allocated to a single unit of this type.
The unique identifier for a unit type within the originating system. Primary key of the PropertyUnitTypes resource.
Aggregate rent across all units of this type (UnitTypeActualRent * UnitTypeUnitsTotal in the simple case).
A single-value lookup describing the unit type: Apartments | Studio | Loft | Penthouse | 1 Bedroom | 2 Bedroom | 3 Bedroom | 4 Bedroom Or More | Efficiency | Manager's Unit.
Total number of units of this type within the parent Property. For example, a 60-unit project with 20 '2 Bedroom' units would set UnitTypeUnitsTotal=20 on the 2 Bedroom row.
The full street address of the property as a single, unparsed string.
The URL of an unbranded virtual tour (no broker identification) for the property. Designed for syndication to third-party portals that do not allow broker branding within tour content.
The year the property was originally constructed.
The effective year built. The year all major renovations were completed making the property feel newer than its original Year Built.
